description: Size and shape overall similar to that of Spotted Towhee (except subtleties of head/bill shape). Upperparts and head the peculiar brown color that is typical of Eastern Towhee (I hate 'milk chocolate' to describe that color). Orange sides. Belly and chest below hood white. Only white markings dorsally were on wings with one white tertial tip and a fully formed white 'box' at base of primaries with a thin line of white on lower primary edge. Head and bill shape that of EATO, being smaller billed and rounder headed than Spotted Towhee.
